Giants' Late-Inning Surge Secures Wild-Card Tie - The San Francisco Giants staged a thrilling comeback in the fourth inning, capped off by Edgardo Alfonzo's go-ahead two-run double, to tie the Chicago Cubs for the wild-card lead after defeating the San Diego Padres, 7-5, in a thrilling matchup last night.

Giants Rally Past Padres, Tie Cubs for Wild-Card Top Spot - The San Francisco Giants' offense came alive in the fourth inning, fueled by a six-run outburst, including a crucial two-run double from Edgardo Alfonzo, to take down the San Diego Padres, 7-5, and move into a tie with the Chicago Cubs for the wild-card lead.

Alfonzo's Clutch Hit Helps Giants Claim Wild-Card Share - The San Francisco Giants' Edgardo Alfonzo delivered a clutch two-run double in the fourth inning to cap off a six-run rally, propelling the team to a 7-5 victory over the San Diego Padres and a share of the wild-card lead with the Chicago Cubs.
